authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 484.60 - Condition of Participation: Care planning, coordination of services, and quality of care
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Federal Register / e-CFR mirrored at Cornell LII
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Direct legal baseline for home health care plans, physician signatures, and care coordination obligations that define execution evidence requirements in home health cases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: care planning, coordination, and execution readiness

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 483.21 - Comprehensive person-centered care planning
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Federal Register / e-CFR mirrored at Cornell LII
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines nursing-facility care-planning and person-centered plan requirements that materially shape case progression and artifact expectations for elder care operations.
used_for: resident and family rights-aware workflow gating

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 418.56 - Hospice condition: interdisciplinary group, care planning, and coordination of services
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Federal Register / e-CFR mirrored at Cornell LII
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Specific hospice care-plan and interdisciplinary coordination rules for hospice-stage workflows, directly tied to end-of-life family/care coordination.

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR § 441.725 - Person-centered service plan
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Federal Register / e-CFR mirrored at Cornell LII
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Explicitly governs person-centered service plans and outcomes evidence in HCBS contexts that overlap elder and disability operations.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Data Set (MDS) 3.0 Resident Assessment Instrument Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Vendor-neutral, recognized operational workflow for SNF resident assessment that feeds care plan and case-artifact sequencing.

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Agencies - Conditions of Participation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Summarizes existing CMS conditions of participation and links to the applicable CFR clauses controlling minimum quality, record, and service expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR § 164.502 - Uses and disclosures of protected health information
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office for Civil Rights (OCR) / e-CFR mirrored at Cornell LII
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Sets federally binding privacy and minimum-necessary constraints for PHI handling and informs refusal/escalation behavior in family/service records workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Case intake validation and dependency capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that case ownership, intake state, and prerequisite artifacts (consent, assessment, plan, and authorized contacts) are present before execution starts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution-state progression
workflow_stages.description: Advance through lifecycle states only when care-plan artifacts and communication evidence are current, complete, and attributable to known owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and conflict handling
workflow_stages.description: Track conflicts from incomplete records, policy source differences, and ownership ambiguity; hold execution and escalate before false completion.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce a closure package showing state transitions, remaining risks, artifact set completeness, and explicit next owner if rework is required.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Comprehensive person-centered care plans or hospice plans of care
Person-centered service plans and individualized goals
Assessment records (e.g., clinical or functional assessment artifacts such as MDS-based inputs)
Family or proxy consents and communication acknowledgements
Case intake records and lifecycle state history
Completion checklist, closure notes, exception queues, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: Nursing facility records and assessment systems

allowed: validate execution readiness from approved care-plan, consent, and assessment records
route clearly to adjacent specialist when a request requires licensed-clinical, legal, or eligibility decision
capture and transmit missing-evidence and conflict queues
escalate: conflicting care-plan authority signals across systems
policy conflicts between tenant program rules and federal baselines
privacy or consent-sensitive escalation requiring legal/risk specialist review
clinical treatment or legal rights questions
refuse: close or mark complete without completion evidence
authorize clinical care, diagnosis, or legal adjudication
operate without minimum required plan-of-care artifacts

domain_failure_modes: Case completion attempted with missing or stale person-centered plan or consent artifacts
Conflicting artifact states across EHR, case management, and scheduling systems
Overgeneralized handling that ignores elder/disability terminology (care goals, hospice review cycle, person-centered services)
Escalation wrongly absorbed by operations lane despite clinical/legal/rights ownership requirement
Privacy/sensitivity failures from exposing PHI or family records without clear authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant policy retrieval is required before case execution in each state or waiver context; no generic assumptions are made without tenant overlay.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact minimum retention period for care-transition records
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Use tenant record-retention policy and facility SOP for retention windows; this lane records retention needs but does not set policy.

notes: No unreviewed assumptions are made on clinical authority; execution control remains within operations and routing decisions.
Evidence review is strict: absence of care plan, consent, or completion artifacts always holds execution or escalates until tenant policy can be confirmed.
The lane stays industry-specific through care-plan, person-centered service planning, family-consent, and end-of-life terminology and does not default to generic operations behavior.
